_meta.note: Built to the Armagh-locked standard. Sligo's season ran five games: a shock Connacht quarter-final loss to lower-division Leitrim, then a four-game Tailteann Cup run (R1 loss to Tipperary, R2B win over Waterford, R3 win over London, quarter-final hammering by Fermanagh).
_meta.verification: All scorelines, venues and referees verified against primary match reports (gaa.ie, RTE, the42, Irish Examiner, Munster GAA, OceanFM) and the official 2026 Tailteann Cup bracket. Full scorer lists and two-pointer counts sourced for the games where a breakdown was published (Tipperary R1, London R3); honest nulls elsewhere.

manager.name: Dessie Sloyan and Eamonn O'Hara
manager.note: Joint-manager arrangement. Sloyan named in match coverage as the spokesman after the Waterford win.

source.snippet: the side co-managed by Dessie Sloyan and Eamonn O'Hara, would normally be the fancy here on paper.

league.note: Division 3 side in 2026, a division above Leitrim (Division 4), which is what made the Connacht quarter-final loss a shock. Exact final table position and round-by-round results not yet sourced — flagged as a gap.

source.snippet: That was a shock result for Sligo, given their Allianz Football League Division 3 status compared to Leitrim, who operated in Division 4 this year.
Fermanagh enjoyed an emphatic win over Sligo, claiming victory by 2-25 to 0-9.

scorers.note: 3 frees, 1 two-pointer; late two-pointer left them one short

source.snippet: Leitrim Sligo Quarter-Final 1-20 3-15 Venue: Markievicz Park, Sligo Referee: Fergal Kelly

results.note: A genuine shock: Sligo (Division 3) lost at home by a single point to Leitrim (Division 4), the result that launched Leitrim's run to the Connacht semi-final. Full scorer list not yet sourced; Leitrim's file will carry the opposing perspective. Referee: Fergal Kelly.

source.snippet: Tipperary defeated Sligo by 0-17 to 1-12 in Round 1 of the Tailteann Cup Football on Sunday May 10th at Markievicz Park Sligo.

results.note: Trailed by seven at half-time; Pat Spillane's two two-pointers off the bench sparked a late rally that fell two short, the equalising efforts (a Murphy two-point free and a Spillane shot) dropping short in the closing minutes. The Sligo two-pointer count is the two from Spillane plus Murphy's noted two-point-free attempt and one more; reported as four two-point attempts converted across the team per the source. Tipperary's four two-pointers (incl. 2 from S. O'Connor) read from the same report. Referee: Kieran Eannetta (Tyrone).

source.snippet: Sligo senior gaelic footballers have safely negotiated their Tailteann Cup round 2B assignment at Fraher Field following their 0-29 to 3-12 win over Waterford on Sunday. Lee Deignan scored 11 points for the winners.

results.note: Last-chance R2B win to stay in the competition. Lee Deignan top-scored with 0-11. Full scorer list and two-pointer breakdown beyond Deignan not sourced. Waterford ended the season 14 months and 14 competitive games without a win.

source.snippet: Sligo booked a place in this year's Tailteann Cup Quarter-Finals as goals from Gavin Gorman and Canice Mulligan proved decisive in Ruislip.

results.note: Two second-half goals (Gorman, Mulligan) settled a tight game in Ruislip; level three times before Sligo pulled away. Niall Murphy made his 150th Sligo appearance. London raised no two-pointers in the report. London's own file will carry the opposing perspective.

scorers.note: substitute; 1 two-point free (last score of the game)
Irish News splits this as Lee Deignan 0-1 plus Shane Deignan 0-1; both versions sum to 0-9

scorers.note: had a 45th-min penalty saved by McNally

source.snippet: Fermanagh enjoyed an emphatic win over Sligo, claiming victory by 2-25 to 0-9. Mattie McDermott hit 1-4 and Shane McGullion scored 1-2, while Ultan Kelm fired 0-4 for the winners.

results.note: Elimination game, a 22-point hammering away to Fermanagh (this game is also recorded in Fermanagh's file from the opposite perspective). Full Sligo scorer list not sourced; the team managed only 0-9.

collection_meta.notes: Sligo's season is a clean five-game arc with a clear emotional shape: a shock home loss to lower-division Leitrim that knocked them out of Connacht, a near-miss against Tipperary, then two wins to claw back to the Tailteann quarter-final before a heavy elimination by Fermanagh. Lee Deignan (0-11 v Waterford, two-pointer v London) and veteran captain Niall Murphy (150th appearance v London) are the names to carry forward. Lower narrative priority than Roscommon or Galway, but a complete and honest record.
